sources.full: Israel P. Warren D.D., The Stanley Families of America (1887)

narrative_blocks.header: Second marriage and death on same day, 1802
narrative_blocks.body: Eleven years after his first wife Mary Sedgwick died in April 1791, he married Widow Eliza Harris Wilcox on 12 September 1802. Edward Hooker's 1909 genealogy records that he died the same day — a date discrepancy that may reflect either an unusual deathbed marriage or a transcription confusion in the source.

narrative_blocks.header: Six in his family fought the Revolution
narrative_blocks.body: Per EH 389, six men of Daniel III's West Hartford household served as Revolutionary War soldiers: his son Daniel (#989, later a pensioner), and the husbands of five daughters — Aaron Cadwell, Timothy Fisher (pensioner), Roger Clark (pensioner), Lemuel Rowell, and Joseph Hubbell. A remarkable concentration of Revolutionary service within one Hooker family.

narrative_blocks.header: Fourteen children with Mary Sedgwick over forty-two years
narrative_blocks.body: He and his first wife Mary Sedgwick — daughter of Stephen Sedgwick — had fourteen documented children between 1749 and approximately 1775. Mary died at West Hartford on 7 April 1791 after forty-two years of marriage.

burial.plot_notes: Green Mount Cemetery, Montpelier, Washington County, Vermont (WikiTree)

military_service.notes: Revolutionary War soldier and pensioner. Physician; never married. Served from West Hartford CT. Became a pensioner — one of three in the family to do so (with Timothy Fisher and Roger Clark). (EH 989)

career.notes: Physician 'like his predecessors' (his father Dr. Daniel III and grandfather Dr. Daniel II of West Hartford). Never married. Died at West Hartford 1842.

narrative_blocks.header: Husband Joseph Hubbell Revolutionary War soldier
narrative_blocks.body: Eunice married Joseph Hubbell in 1791. Joseph Hubbell had served as a Revolutionary War soldier, listed among the sons-in-law fighting in the Continental cause alongside Eunice's other brothers-in-law Timothy Fisher, Roger Clark, Lemuel Rowell, and Aaron Cadwell. The Hooker-line cluster of Revolutionary veterans marked the family's contribution to the war from its Hartford Connecticut base.

narrative_blocks.header: Four children including daughter Eunice Hubbell
narrative_blocks.body: Eunice and Joseph Hubbell had four documented children, including a daughter Eunice Hubbell who carried forward the maternal name into the next generation. The four-child family was modest by late-eighteenth-century standards and may reflect undocumented infant losses or simply a smaller completed family size.

narrative_blocks.header: Hartford 1772 to West Haven 1851 age seventy-eight
narrative_blocks.body: Eunice Hooker was born December 25, 1772 in Hartford Connecticut — daughter of Daniel Hooker (the family's third Daniel) and Mary Sedgwick Hooker — and died December 9, 1851 in West Haven Connecticut at age seventy-eight. The Hartford-to-coastal-Connecticut life arc tracked her marriage migration south to the Hubbell family's coastal New Haven County settlement.

burial.plot_notes: Old Center Cemetery (also called North Cemetery), West Hartford CT. Gravestone: "HENRY BRACE & ABIGAIL his wife... She died Aug.07, 1802. EA. 5_." SOURCE: Hale Collection.

narrative_blocks.header: Hooker name preserved as middle name across generations
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Hooker married Henry Brace, and the Hooker surname survived as a middle name through her children, grandchildren, and beyond — a deliberate preservation through collateral lines. Examples include her son Edmond Hooker Brace, her grandson Hezekiah Hooker Brace, and her grandchildren Abigail Hooker Pelton and Edmund Hooker Pelton through her daughter Abigail Hooker Brace Pelton. The pattern preserves Hooker identity across family branches even as primary surnames change.

parents.father_research_notes: Capt. Henry Brace of West Hartford CT (per Findagrave). EH 396 calls him simply 'Henry Brace'; the 'Captain' title comes from Findagrave/Old Center Cemetery records.
parents.mother_research_notes: Elizabeth Cadwell Brace of West Hartford CT — Cadwell is the same West Hartford family Aaron Cadwell (X00910, husband of Mary Hooker H00986) descends from. The Brace and Cadwell families intermarried with the Hookers across multiple generations.

sources.short: WikiTree — Henry Brace III + Abigail (Hooker) Brace profiles
Findagrave — Pvt Henry Brace III VETERAN (Old Center Cemetery, West Hartford CT)
Connecticut Rolls and Lists Vol XIII p.234

narrative_blocks.body: He married Abigail Hooker at Hartford in 1763 and they had seventeen documented children between then and approximately 1788. Edward Hooker's 1909 genealogy entries 1022-1037 list the children — though crowdsourced records add and subtract from this number in places.

narrative_blocks.header: Revolutionary War private from West Hartford
narrative_blocks.body: He mustered as a private in the Revolutionary War from West Hartford — alongside thousands of other Connecticut farmers and tradesmen who left family homesteads for the Continental ranks. He survived the war and returned to West Hartford where he lived another three decades.

narrative_blocks.header: Widowed twelve years before his 1814 death
narrative_blocks.body: His wife Abigail Hooker died at West Hartford on 8 August 1802 after thirty-nine years of marriage. He lived twelve more years alone at West Hartford before his own death on 17 March 1814 at age sixty-nine.

burial.plot_notes: Old Center Cemetery, West Hartford CT. Buried in row 13 (per Findagrave + WikiTree).

military_service.unit: 4th Company, Connecticut Troops, under Lieut. Charles Seymour
military_service.notes: Enlisted Aug 15, 1776 in the 4th Company, Connecticut Troops under Lieut. Charles Seymour. Marched toward New York Aug 16, 1776 — set sail from New Haven and arrived in New York. Date of discharge not known. Sources: Connecticut Rolls and Lists Vol XIII p.234; West Hartford Church Records; West Hartford Cemetery Records p.41.

burial.plot_notes: Old Center Cemetery, West Hartford CT. Shared gravestone with Abigail.

narrative_blocks.body: Edmund Brace was born October 7, 1768, baptized two days later, and died October 15 — eight days old.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

narrative_blocks.header: Two children married into one Deming sibling pair
narrative_blocks.body: Lory Sr's son Thomas Brace married Electa Alling Deming in 1823, and his daughter Eliza Ann Brace married Walter Deming in 1827. Electa and Walter were brother-and-sister, both children of Theron and Electa Ensign Deming of West Hartford. Two Brace siblings married two Deming siblings within four years — a classic small-town Connecticut close-network pattern.

narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Elizabeth Brace was born in 1772 and died December 6, 1772, in West Hartford.

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

narrative_blocks.header: Named for her mother Abigail Hooker Brace
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail Hooker Brace (b. 1774) carried both her mother's given name and her mother's maiden name Hooker — a deliberate naming tribute to the line.

narrative_blocks.header: Bore twin daughters Cynthia and Sylvia
narrative_blocks.body: Abigail and her husband Seth Pelton had twin daughters born May 23, 1793 in Litchfield New York: Cynthia and Sylvia. Both grew to adulthood. Sylvia married Nathan Beardsley; Cynthia is starred in EH (sub-entry has further family detail not yet enumerated in our records).

sources.label: Edward Hooker, Descendants of Rev. Thomas Hooker (1909)

narrative_blocks.header: Three of his first four children died young
narrative_blocks.body: Manning Brace and Lucy Webster lost three of their first four children: Mahala (b.1796) died age 5 in 1802; their first Lucy (b. Jan 1798) lived only 30 days; their first Fanny (b.1799) died age 5 in 1804. The fourth, Lucy Mahala (b.1801), lived to 1865 — given a name combining her two lost sisters' names.

narrative_blocks.header: Three children married into the Edgecomb family
narrative_blocks.body: Of Riverius Jr's ten children, three married Edgecombs in close succession: daughter Mehitable married Joseph Edgecomb in 1834; son Samuel married Rhoda Edgecomb in 1823; daughter Emma Jane married the same Joseph Edgecomb in 1867 as his second wife after her sister Mehitable's death. Two Hooker sisters, one Edgecomb husband.
